# Plan: RQ-0859 - Restrict Model type to known values in schema

## Task Goal

Update the JSON schema for the `Model` type to enumerate known model identifiers while allowing custom model strings. This provides IDE autocomplete, catches typos at config validation time, and documents the supported models.

## Why

The Model schema was just `"type": "string"` with no constraints. Typos like `sonet` instead of valid values pass schema validation silently but fail at runtime when the model identifier is unrecognized by the AI runner.

## Status: IMPLEMENTED (Prior Iteration)

The implementation was completed in a prior iteration. The working tree has uncommitted changes that implement the full solution.

### What Was Implemented

| File | Change | Status |
|------|--------|--------|
| `crates/ralph/src/contracts/model.rs` | Updated `json_schema()` to generate `oneOf` with known models | DONE |
| `schemas/config.schema.json` | Regenerated with new Model schema | DONE |
| `schemas/queue.schema.json` | Regenerated with new Model schema | DONE |
| `crates/ralph/src/contracts/model.rs` | Added test `model_json_schema_includes_known_models` | DONE |

### Implementation Details

The `json_schema()` implementation (lines 86-127) now generates:

```json
{
  "oneOf": [
    {"type": "string", "const": "gpt-5.3-codex", "description": "OpenAI GPT-5.3 Codex (default)"},
    {"type": "string", "const": "gpt-5.3-codex-spark", "description": "OpenAI GPT-5.3 Codex Spark (fast)"},
    {"type": "string", "const": "gpt-5.3", "description": "OpenAI GPT-5.3"},
    {"type": "string", "const": "gpt-5.2-codex", "description": "OpenAI GPT-5.2 Codex"},
    {"type": "string", "const": "gpt-5.2", "description": "OpenAI GPT-5.2"},
    {"type": "string", "const": "zai-coding-plan/glm-4.7", "description": "ZhipuAI GLM-4.7"},
    {"type": "string", "description": "Custom model identifier", "minLength": 1}
  ]
}
```

## Phase 2: Execution Tasks

**The implementation is already in the working tree.** Phase 2 should:

1. **Run CI verification:**
   ```bash
   make macos-ci
   ```

2. **Verify test passes:**
   - Test `model_json_schema_includes_known_models` validates the schema structure

3. **If CI passes, the changes are ready to commit.**

## Verification Checklist

- [x] `json_schema()` generates `oneOf` with 6 known models
- [x] Custom model fallback has `minLength: 1` constraint
- [x] Both schemas (`config.schema.json` and `queue.schema.json`) updated
- [x] Unit test added for schema structure
- [ ] `make macos-ci` passes (Phase 2 verification)

## Notes

- The `Custom(String)` variant in the enum already handles arbitrary model strings at runtime
- Empty strings are already rejected by `Model::from_str()` (lines 65-67)
- Both schemas share the same Model definition via `$defs/Model` generated from the same Rust type
